title: Anthosachne × elymoides
taxonomy: Anthosachne × elymoides is a hybrid species in the family Poaceae. It is a member of the grass family, specifically belonging to the subfamily Danthonioideae. Within this subfamily, Anthosachne × elymoides is placed in the tribe Danthonieae. Further classification places it in the genus Anthosachne, which is a monotypic genus consisting of this single hybrid species. The full classification of Anthosachne × elymoides is therefore as follows: Anthosachne × elymoides (A. exilis × Danthonia sp.) is classified as: Anthosachne × elymoides, family Poaceae, subfamily Danthonioideae, tribe Danthonieae, genus Anthosachne.
uses: Anthosachne × elymoides has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Australia where it is native. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and wounds. It is also believed to possess antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating infections. In some regions, the plant is used to make a tea that is consumed to aid in digestion. Further research is needed to confirm the full range of its medicinal uses.

intro: Anthosachne × elymoides, commonly known as a hybrid of the Anthosachne and Elymus species, is a type of perennial grass native to Australia and New Zealand. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and reducing the risk of infection. The bioactive constituents of Anthosachne × elymoides include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, as well as topical creams and ointments, which are used to treat various skin conditions and promote tissue repair.
